items.title: Raining Bicycles
items.description: Released in 1996 on the Ceirnini Cladagh label, this 13-track album features the eclectic Dublin-based band Skylark with Mairtin O'Connor on accordions, Garry O'Briain on guitars and keyboards, Gerry O'Connor on fiddles and viola, and Len Graham on vocals. The album's distinctive title comes from an incident in Leipzig, Germany where the band was warned of bicycles being hurled from above. The collection showcases energy, joy and musical creativity.

items.title: At our Leisure
items.description: A banjo and uilleann pipes collaboration released on 23 October 2022. John Carty plays banjo, fiddle and flute, while Michael McGoldrick plays uilleann pipes, flute, bodhrán and low whistle. The two formed their first collaboration at a concert celebrating Irish President Michael D. Higgins's state visit to London at the Royal Albert Hall in 2014. This 17-track album is supported by Matt Griffin on guitar and features bouzouki and guitar accompaniment.

items.title: Heartstring Sessions
items.description: A 2008 release on Old Bridge Music featuring guitarist Arty McGlynn, violinist Nollaig Casey, harper Máire Ní Chathasaigh, and flat-picking guitarist Chris Newman. This eclectic mix combines studio and live tracks with traditional tunes, songs and new compositions featuring everything from sparkling Irish dance-music and gorgeous airs to bluegrass and ragtime rockabilly. The musicians' contributions have influenced everyone from Van Morrison to Riverdance.

items.title: The Kane Sisters
items.description: The fourth album by Liz and Yvonne Kane from North Connemara, released in August 2022. Titled 'In Memory of Paddy Fahey', this album features 15 of the master composer's compositions along with newly written tunes from both sisters. The Kane Sisters were taught music by Mary Finn and their grandfather Jimmy Mullen, and were influenced by Sligo and East Galway traditions. They have won the women's competition at Oireachtas na Gaeilge multiple times.

items.title: Keane Connolly McGorman
items.description: A traditional Irish music album released in 2023 on Raelach Records. This collection represents the collaborative effort of musicians from three renowned Irish music families.

items.title: The Queen of Mayo
items.description: The debut album by The Piper's Cross Céilí Band, released on 15 July 2024. The band was crowned 2023 All-Ireland Senior Céilí Band Champions at The All Ireland Fleadh 2023 in Mullingar - the first ever band from Mayo to win this title since the first Fleadh Cheoil in 1951. The 12-piece band features musicians from throughout County Mayo.

items.title: From Both Sides
items.description: The debut solo album from fiddle and concertina player Niamh Ní Charra, released on 1 March 2007. From Killarney and steeped in Sliabh Luachra musical traditions, Ní Charra spent 8 years touring the world as the fiddler with Riverdance before returning to Ireland. This 13-track collection was the only Irish album to make MOJO's Top Ten Folk Albums for 2007. The album features contributions from Brendan Begley, Mike Galvin, Robbie Harris, and others.

items.title: Queen of Connemara
items.description: Released in July 2019, this album from sean-nós singer Caitríona Ní Cheannabháin features the collection 'Queen of Connemara – Songs from the Mist'. From Carna in the heart of Connemara, Caitríona has been singing from childhood and is a three-time winner of the women's competition at Oireachtas na Gaeilge. The album includes favourite well-known sean nós songs like 'An Spailpín Fánach' and 'Tá mo Chleamhnas Déanta'.

items.title: Musical Disciples of Coleman
items.description: A Various Artists compilation featuring 'The Musical Disciples Of Coleman The Great', released in 2017. The album celebrates the legacy of legendary fiddle player Michael Coleman and features the Coleman Country Céilí Band and various North Connaught traditional musicians, preserving and honoring the Coleman musical tradition.

items.title: A Musical Trip in Time
items.description: A nostalgic collection of music played on harmonica by Gerard Donagher, released in 2013. This double CD set includes CD 1 with traditional Irish tunes and airs, and CD 2 featuring popular melodies. Born and reared in Kilmactranny, County Sligo, in a community steeped in Irish Traditional Music, Donagher shares his musical journey through harmonica interpretation.

items.title: Vol 1 U.S. Recordings
items.description: A compilation album of various artists' recordings released on the Oldtime Records label.

items.title: Coming of Spring
items.description: A concert recording from a traditional music course in 2007, capturing live performances from students and instructors.

items.title: Ceol sa Chistin
items.description: A concert recording from a traditional music course in 2014, featuring live performances from course participants.

items.title: Jimmys Other Hall
items.description: A concert recording from a traditional music course in 2015, featuring live performances. This album includes both concert photos.

items.title: Ceol Rising
items.description: A concert recording from a traditional music course in 2016, featuring live performances from course participants.

items.title: Ceolchoirm - A Music Legacy
items.description: A concert recording from a traditional music course in 2017, capturing live performances and celebrating the musical legacy of Irish traditional music education.

items.title: Littoral
items.description: A collection of traditional Irish music from Leonard Barry.

items.title: Suas Leat
items.description: A collection of ceili band music from the Swallows Tail Céilí Band.

items.title: Irish Songs we learned at school
items.description: A collection of traditional Irish songs and melodies from musician John Spillane.

items.title: Here to Play
items.description: A collection of traditional Irish music from Bernadette Nic Gabhann.

items.title: From Ireland to Boston
items.description: A collection of music from St Michael's Scout Brass Band, representing musical traditions from Ireland to Boston.

items.title: and all points west
items.description: A collection of music from Frank Cullen, exploring musical traditions across various regions.

items.title: Tin Whistles
items.description: A collection of tin whistle music from Paddy Maloney and Sean Potts.

items.title: Fleadh Ceili Bands
items.description: A compilation of recordings from Fleadh Cheoil ceili band competitions, featuring various traditional Irish ceili bands.

items.title: Dance Upon the Mountains
items.description: The third album by The Dartry Céilí Band from County Sligo, released in 2023. This 13-track collection features a mix of reels, jigs, barndances, hornpipes, polkas, and two songs sung by band members Noelle Carroll and Cian Kearins. Over 42 minutes of energetic ceili band music celebrating Irish traditional dance.

items.title: Are We There Yet?
items.description: A folk album by Cora Smyth released in 2008, featuring 12 songs of Irish folk music with traditional and contemporary arrangements.

items.title: Live in Galway
items.description: A live recording by We Banjo 3 captured over two nights in February 2015 at the Roisin Dubh in Galway. This 15-track collection features guest performances by JigJam and The Whileaways, showcasing the band's energetic blend of banjos, fiddle, accordion, and dobro in a celebration of Irish and American traditional music.
trackList: Sail Away Ladies / Because It's There
We All Need More Kindness In This World
Bill Cheatum / Kitchen Girl / Donegal Lass

items.title: Heart Strings
items.description: An album featuring Colm O'Donnell, a multi-instrumentalist from Monalea in Sligo known for his mastery of flute, whistle, and fiddle. One of the greatest traditional singers of his time with a record number of All-Ireland titles.

items.title: In Memory of Paddy Fahey
items.description: A tribute album by Liz and Yvonne Kane dedicated to master composer and fiddle player Paddy Fahey. Released in August 2022, this 14-track collection features fifteen of Fahey's compositions alongside newly composed tunes from the sisters, expertly accompanied by John Blake on guitar and keyboards, Neil Martin on cello, and step-dancer Nathan Pilatzke.

items.description: An album by Donal O'Connor from 2001 featuring traditional Irish music.

items.title: Live and Kicking
items.description: A double CD celebrating 50 years of Shaskeen making music together. Released in April 2020, this live recording features 24 tracks of traditional Irish music and song captured at two of their concerts, showcasing their signature céilí band style with an incredibly tight interweaving of instruments.

items.title: There's Always Room in our House
items.description: A duet album featuring banjo and sax as the core instruments. Tom Cussen plays tenor banjo, mandolin, and tenor guitar, while Tony Howley plays saxophone, concert flute, and tin whistle. The collection includes reels, jigs, old time and quicksteps.

items.title: Funny Songs
items.description: An album by Con O'Driscoll featuring humorous traditional Irish songs.

items.title: Ballinafad
items.description: An album by the McDonaghs featuring traditional Irish music.

items.title: At Their Best
items.description: A recording transferred from 33 RPM vinyl, featuring Roger Sherlock and Seán Maguire with Josephine Keegan from 1971.

items.title: The Cat's Meow
items.description: An album by The Cat's Meow.

items.title: Welcome to the Hotel Connemara
items.description: A creative 2000 release by De Dannan featuring rock and roll covers given the full traditional Irish treatment. This 15-track album showcases the band's versatility, transforming classic rock songs with their signature bouzouki, fiddle, whistle, flute, accordion, and other traditional instruments into vibrant Irish arrangements.
trackList: Rocks of Bawn / Take It to the Limit
When a Man Loves a Woman

items.title: Return of Spring Concert
items.description: A charity concert recording from a traditional music course on March 14, 2008.

items.title: Tíanam Ort! Come Away
items.description: An album featuring songs and music from Muiris (Mossie) O'Scanlán's two lands, released in 2003.

items.title: Fliúit 2
items.description: A follow-on to June McCormack's 2006 flute tutorial, Fliúit 2 is suitable for intermediate and advanced flute players. This 2009 tutorial focuses on developing repertoire, ornamentation, and breathing techniques with two accompanying CDs containing 78 instructional tracks.

items.title: Live at the Moving Stairs
items.description: A live album by Donal O'Connor recorded at the Moving Stairs in 2005.

items.title: Back in Time
items.description: A collaboration between Angelina Carberry and Dan Brouder released in 2021.

items.title: The Banks of the Shannon
items.description: A restored and remastered recording from the original 1973 release featuring Paddy O'Brien, Seamus Connolly, and Charlie Lennon.

items.title: Shackleton's Endurance
items.description: A musical account of the story of Shackleton's Endurance expedition, released in 2014.

items.title: On The Off Beat
items.description: Liz Carroll's first solo album in 11 years, released in 2013. Comprised of all original tunes except for The Yellow Tinker, this album celebrates quirky, non-conformist rhythms that revel in the unpredictability of Balkan influences and the buoyant agility of the Sligo fiddle style. Produced by Seamus Egan with guest musicians including Catriona McKay on harp, Trevor Hutchinson on bass, Seán Óg Graham on guitar, Natalie Haas on cello, and Winifred Horan on fiddle.

items.title: The Macalla Suite
items.description: A live recording from Monaghan in October 2016 featuring The Macalla Orchestra. The Macalla Suite is a musical commemoration of the Easter 1916 Rising, consisting of sixteen original compositions by Michael Rooney and three traditional songs. Recorded with around sixty musicians on stage, this album was released in December 2016.

items.title: Lane to the Glen
items.description: A delightful, joyful and heartfelt homage to the rich tradition of Irish music from County Sligo, released on July 1, 2024. This 13-track album revisits rare tunes from the early to mid-20th century, capturing the transatlantic influence and celebrating Sligo's golden age of Irish traditional music. Features Oisín Mac Diarmada on fiddle, Daithí Gormley on accordion, and Samantha Harvey on piano.

items.title: Once Was
items.description: An album by Robert John Ardiff.

items.title: Epiphany
items.description: A remarkable solo debut by Elaine Reilly released on June 1, 2024, featuring Irish traditional music on the tenor banjo. This 14-track collection showcases Reilly's expressive mix of old and new tunes with attention to detail inspired by past masters. Joined by Brian McGrath on piano, Brian Mooney on bouzouki, and Daithí Gormley on accordion/melodeon, Reilly demonstrates why she is one of the foremost tenor banjo players of her generation, having won the senior All-Ireland in 2014.

items.title: Now and in Time To Be
items.description: A musical celebration of the works of WB Yeats from 1992.

items.title: Box & Banjo
items.description: An album by accordion/keyboard player Kenny Donagher from Easkey, Sligo and banjo/mandolin player Kerrie Herrity from Maugherow. Kerrie, a former All Ireland Banjo Senior Champion, brings compositions inspired by the beautiful surroundings of Sligo to this collaboration.

items.title: The Waylaid Man
items.description: An album by Michael McCague released in 2014.

items.title: Fourmilehouse
items.description: A duet recording by brothers Alan Kelly (piano accordion) and John Kelly (flute and whistle) released on May 7, 2002. Recorded at Sun Street Studios in Tuam, Co. Galway, this album features traditional tunes played with accompaniment by guitarists and other instrumentalists. The Kelly brothers are from Roscommon and named the album after Fourmilehouse in southern Roscommon, where their father Frank Kelly, a piano accordionist, won the All-Ireland senior title in 1964.

items.title: Ten
items.description: An album celebrating 10 years of traditional music from the heart, released in 2015.

items.title: The Wishing Tree
items.description: The first studio album by the trio of John Doyle (guitar), John McCusker (fiddle/whistle), and Mike McGoldrick (flute/whistle/uilleann pipes), released in 2017. This album celebrates their friendship and musical kinship a quarter century after they first crossed paths as teenagers. Largely recorded in four days, it includes a mixture of tunes they've been playing together, brand new tunes, and traditional songs spanning hundreds of years.

items.title: Rock & Reel
items.description: A 1998 release by Montreal-based La Bottine Souriante, their 10th CD, which combines their talents with three guest World Music performers. This 13-track, 51:48 minute album takes traditional Musique Québécoise to new heights, with brass, percussion, and reeds perfectly blended into traditional French North American gigues and reels, melds traditional roots music with contemporary jazz.

items.title: Bad Turns & Horse-shoe Bends
items.description: An album by Harry Bradley released in 2000 on the Outlet label.

items.title: Irish Childhood Songs
items.description: An album of Irish childhood songs released in 2010 on the Gaellinn label.

items.title: Paddy McMenamin & Friends
items.description: An album by Paddy McMenamin and friends released in 2016.

items.title: The Joy of It
items.description: An album by Bad to the Bow released in 2012.

items.title: When I Was Young
items.description: A collaborative album of children's songs from Ireland released in 1999. This balanced repertoire features timeless songs such as Rosemary Fair, The Wheel of Fortune, I Once Had a Granny, I Know Where I'm Going, The False Knight, As I Roved Out, and many others. Len Graham is a Northern Irish traditional singer from County Antrim and leading authority on Irish folk music who won the All Ireland Fleadh Cheoil na hÉireann traditional singing competition in 1971.

items.title: Retrospective
items.description: A recording by Ceoltóir, students of the Higher National Diploma in Irish Traditional Music Performance at Ballyfermot College of Further Education, released in 2007.

items.title: Lady's Cup of Tea
items.description: An album by Mary McNamara and her daughter.

items.title: Christmas Bells
items.description: A 2024 single by Aoife Duignan featuring Mossie Martin.

items.title: Cormac Begley
items.description: A self-titled debut solo album by Cormac Begley released on December 21, 2017. This 13-track, 36-minute collection features Begley playing eight different concertinas spanning seven octaves. The album contains a mixture of traditional and newly composed tunes, released as a limited edition with a unique hexagonal case design. Begley is a bass, baritone, treble, and piccolo concertina player from a west-Kerry musical family whose debut album received critical acclaim with nine 5-star reviews.

items.title: Fiddle Music
items.description: The debut solo album from Dublin fiddle player Danny Diamond released on July 6, 2014. This 12-track album showcases Diamond's unique and distinctive fiddle style built on influences from Dublin fiddlers Tommie Potts and Seán Keane, his parents' music, the fiddle tradition of Co. Donegal, and American old-time and Nordic fiddle music. The album features tunes from the Irish traditional repertoire alongside five of Danny's compositions. All accompaniment uses drone-based instruments including fiddle, nyckelharpa, uilleann pipes, and accordion. The Irish Times gave it a 5-star review, describing it as 'ambitious and authoritative' and a 'definite contender for CD of the year.'

items.description: A collection of old and new, Irish and Scottish traditional music from a personal selection by Fintan Vallely (flute) and Mark Simos (guitar). Originally issued as a cassette by Whinstone, Dublin in 1992, it was reissued as a CD in 2008. This selection arose out of Vallely's playing in Scotland with singer Tim Lyons in the 1980s. The album features 12 tracks of instrumental traditional music.

items.title: A Flick of the Wrist
items.description: An album by Brendan Larrissey recorded at West One Studios in Galway in September/October 1993 and released in October 1994. Larrissey, from Dundalk, County Louth, won the Senior Fiddle Championship in 1987 and was a founding member of the award-winning band Arcady in 1988. This 13-track, approximately 46-minute album features traditional Irish music with various instrumental collaborators including bodhran, bouzouki, guitar, mandolin, and other traditional instruments.

items.title: Solid Ground
items.description: An album by banjo and tenor guitar player Shane Mulchrone released on February 23, 2018. Produced by Jack Talty and Shane Mulchrone, recorded, mixed, and mastered between 2014 and 2015 in Raelach. This 13-track, 48-minute album features Shane Mulchrone on banjo and tenor guitar, Jack Talty on piano and bass concertina, Noel O'Grady on bouzouki, and Heather Cole-Mullen on melodeon. Mulchrone, from near Ballina in county Mayo, has been playing banjo for over 20 years and cites John Carty and Tommy Finn as early influences.

items.title: After the Ball
items.description: Arcady's debut album released in 1991. This 13-track collection of Celtic and Irish folk music was recorded at Windmill Lane Recording Studio in Dublin. Arcady was formed by bodhran, bones, and percussion player Johnny 'Ringo' McDonagh shortly after leaving De Dannan in 1989. Initially an eight-piece group, Arcady has gone through numerous personnel changes with illustrious alumni including Sharon Shannon, Frances Black, Seán Keane, and Jackie Daly.

items.description: An album by Brenda McCann.

items.title: Temple Bare
items.description: An album by Jenny Fitzgibbon released in 2000.

items.title: Out of the Ashes
items.description: An album released in 2016 featuring flute player Matt Molloy and fiddle player John Carty with guitarist Arty McGlynn. The collaboration includes Noel O'Grady on bouzouki and Brian McGrath on piano. The musicians met at a pub one winter's evening and realized they had very similar musical tastes, particularly the music of previous generations from their locality. The album title 'Out of the Ashes' comes from a saying by Johnny Watt Henry, a great musician known for keeping musicians late into the night.

items.title: Heritage Hall
items.description: An album by Back West released in 2018.

items.title: I gCnoc na Graí
items.description: A landmark album featuring concertinist Noel Hill and button accordionist Tony MacMahon playing together in unison for the first time. Recorded live in October at Dan Connell's pub in Knocknagree, Co. Cork, this album showcases traditional Irish instrumental music for dancing. The collaboration between these two master musicians represents a significant moment in Irish traditional music, combining the delicate concertina sound with the full-bodied accordion.
trackList: The Ace and Deuce of Piping

items.title: Draw the Bow
items.description: Malachy Bourke's debut solo record released in 2001, featuring traditional Irish fiddle music with minimal backing from his father Brian Bourke on bodhrán. A thirteen-track collection of classic Irish reels, jigs, and traditional dance music. Bourke studied under the legendary Frankie Gavin and plays Paddy Killoran's fiddle, bringing deep respect for tradition to this accomplished performance.

items.title: Beautiful Affair
items.description: Siobhán O'Donnell's debut solo album released in September 2016, featuring a beautiful blend of songs and tunes in the South Sligo traditional style. Launched at the November Trad Fest in Ennis 2016, the album showcases her talents as a singer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist. An All-Ireland bodhran champion and award-winning performer, O'Donnell brings her distinctive voice and instrumental prowess to this collection.

items.title: Queen of the Gypsies
items.description: A remastered 2012 release of Margaret Barry's classic recordings, the legendary Irish Traveller and street singer performing timeless Irish traditional songs in her distinctive and evocative style. Barry (1917–1989) was an iconic performer who brought Irish folk music to audiences across Ireland and Britain. This collection showcases her powerful voice and deep connection to Irish musical traditions.

items.title: Tribute
items.description: Jim McKillop's tribute album showcasing his mastery of the Irish fiddle through a diverse collection of traditional and classical pieces. Released in 2008, this 24-track album features hornpipes, reels, strathspeys, waltzes, and airs from the Irish and Scottish traditions. McKillop's accomplished playing demonstrates the technical and emotional depth of traditional fiddle music, with pieces ranging from classical arrangements to spirited traditional dance tunes.

items.title: Fiddlewings
items.description: Manus McGuire's second solo fiddle recording released in May 2006 on Compass Records, showcasing his mastery of Irish traditional fiddle music. Influenced by his love of music from Ireland, Scotland, Shetland, and Cape Breton, this 15-track album demonstrates McGuire's technical skill and musical depth. A winner of the prestigious Fiddler of Dooney competition at age 14, McGuire brings classical training and traditional sensibilities to this accomplished collection.

items.title: Sweeping the Cobwebs out of the Sky
items.description: Fergus McGorman's debut solo album released in 2017, showcasing his masterful playing of the traditional wooden flute. This 13-track collection features 22 tunes performed in the South Sligo style, with accompaniment from pianist Paddy McEvoy, bouzouki player Ruairí McGorman, and guitarist John Blake. A breath of fresh air that demonstrates McGorman's musical maturity and distinctive voice in Irish traditional music.

items.title: Clan Ranald
items.description: A sparkling 2005 debut collaboration between accordion master Martin Tourish (of Altan) and bouzouki player Luke Ward, drawing influences from the northwest of Ireland and the Donegal fiddle tradition. This 13-track album features compositions by Tourish alongside contemporary tunes and traditional favorites, enhanced by guest appearances from accomplished musicians. A critically acclaimed release that topped best-of-2005 lists.

items.title: Stormy Weather
items.description: Beginish's second studio album released in 2001, featuring a talented ensemble of Irish traditional musicians. The 12-track collection showcases a mix of traditional reels, jigs, polkas, and songs in the classic Irish style. With members including fiddler Paul O'Shaughnessy, flute and whistle player Paul McGrattan, accordionist and vocalist Brendan Begley, and a strong rhythm section, the album captures the spirit of traditional Irish music.

items.title: Duopoly
items.description: A collaborative harp duet album featuring Cormac de Barra and Anne-Marie O'Farrell performing on gut-strung, wire-strung, and pedal harps. The album showcases newly composed traditional tunes, fresh duetting arrangements, and includes works by classical composers such as Carolan. Mastered and engineered by Fionán De Barra.

Guest musicians include her sisters on flute, accordion, and bodhrán, plus accompaniment from Paul Meehan on guitar and Brian McGrath on piano.

items.description: A fiddle album by Seamus Gibson, a highly-regarded Donegal fiddler from a renowned musical family. Gibson is the nephew of famed fiddler Tommy Peoples and son of noted mandolin and fiddle player Eddie Gibson.

items.title: Fly By Night
items.description: A live recording featuring concert performances by Seamus Gibson on fiddle and his long-time playing partner Dermot Toland on guitar. This is one of two highly-anticipated first solo CDs from the highly-regarded Donegal fiddler.

items.title: P.J. Hernon
items.description: P.J. Hernon is an accordion player from Carna in Connemara who has won multiple All-Ireland titles. His eponymous debut album on melodeon and accordion was released by Gael Linn as a groundbreaking solo work in traditional Irish music.

items.title: Light Flight
items.description: A compilation anthology featuring 31 songs by British folk rock group Pentangle, highlighting their influential contributions to folk and rock fusion music.

items.title: Unbuttoned
items.description: Tommie Cunniffe's debut solo album released in 2007, featuring 14 tracks of reels, jigs, polkas, and hornpipes. The album showcases many of Cunniffe's own compositions along with traditional tunes, accompanied by guitarist Brian Miller from Minnesota and percussionist Brian Morrisey. Cunniffe firmly established himself as one of the country's leading accordion players with this release.

items.title: In the Wake of Neil Gunn
items.description: An ambitious music-maritime project by Mike Vass that draws inspiration from Scottish writer Neil Gunn's book 'Off In A Boat' about a 1937 voyage along the west coast of Scotland. Defying categorization, the album blends folk and classical styles with diverse instruments including toy piano and clock tower chimes. Highly acclaimed with critical praise from Financial Times, Songlines, and R2 magazine.

items.title: The New Broom
items.description: A traditional Irish music album released in 2009 featuring Willie Kelly on fiddle, Mike Rafferty on flute, and Dónal Clancy. The album contains 17 songs with a duration of 51 minutes, showcasing the accomplished musicianship of these traditional players.

items.description: A traditional Irish music recording featuring whistle player Angela Deane from County Mayo. An All-Ireland Fleadh Cheoil winner in 1995, Deane is joined on this album by Caroline Deane on bodhrán, Frank Kilkelly on guitar and piano, and Paul O'Driscoll on bass. The album contains 17 tracks of traditional Irish music.

items.title: Musique Yann Tiersen - Destin d'Amélie
items.description: The soundtrack album 'Le Fabuleux Destin d'Amélie Poulain' from the 2001 film of the same title by Jean-Pierre Jeunet. This instrumental album by composer Yann Tiersen features a blend of newly composed pieces and rearranged selections from his previous work, with about half derived from earlier works and the rest composed specifically for the film.

Includes compositions by Tommy Makem alongside classic arrangements of traditional songs.

The album showcases tastefully restrained accompaniment from Kevin Brehony on piano and Hugh Sullivan on bodhrán, plus Declan Courell on guitar. Notable features include Duffy's original compositions, particularly two waltzes dedicated to the memory of Countess Markievicz.

Gearóid is a former member of the renowned Kilfenora Ceili Band. The album presents traditional music from the west of Ireland at moderate tempos.

items.title: The First Turn
items.description: Daoirí Farrell's debut album released on June 1, 2009, showcasing 10 tracks of traditional Irish folk music. Farrell is a folk singer and talented player of Irish bouzouki, guitar, and banjo from Dublin. The album went on to lead to his breakthrough second album 'True Born Irishman' in 2016, which won prestigious BBC Radio 2 Folk Awards.

items.title: Legacy

items.description: A selection of songs and tunes with a Cavan connection which shows the varied musical styles of traditional Irish music. Features a diverse range of traditional performers and styles.

items.description: A double CD collection featuring 83 of Josephine Keegan's original compositions and arrangements. Almost three hours of music showcasing the prolific output of this renowned traditional musician.

items.title: Within a Mile O' Jamestown
items.description: A solo flute album by Mick Mulvey featuring 15 tracks of traditional Irish music. Recorded in London, the album showcases Mulvey's performances on both Eb and D flutes with accompaniment from skilled musicians.

items.title: The Calm After the Storm
items.description: Traditional tunes on saxophone performed by Keith Donald. An exploration of traditional Irish music through the lens of the saxophone.

items.title: In Full Flight
items.description: Traditional tunes on accordion and whistle performed by James Donohoe. Showcasing skilful interpretations of Irish traditional music on these classic instruments.

items.title: Máirín Fahy; Featured Soloist from Riverdance
items.description: An album featuring Máirín Fahy, a featured soloist from the world-renowned Riverdance production. Showcasing her talents as a performer of Irish traditional music.

items.title: Sets in Our Way
items.description: A collection of traditional Irish music tunes and sets played on various instruments. Drónán's interpretation of classic and traditional arrangements.

items.description: Le Vent du Nord's eighth album released in 2015, featuring 15 tracks of Québécois traditional music and original compositions. The title 'Têtu' means 'stubborn' in French. The album won the 2015 Félix Award for Traditional Album of the Year.

items.description: A collection of Irish traditional music by Irish Corner, a collection of musicians.

items.description: Gavin Whelan's acclaimed 2001 self-titled album featuring 15 tracks of traditional Irish music. Whelan is one of Ireland's finest tin whistle players, known for his clear tone and precise phrasing, and is also accomplished on the uilleann pipes.

items.description: A 2008 release of Irish traditional music by Tuatha, featuring tunes and songs rooted in the traditional Irish music heritage. The album showcases the band's interpretation of classic Irish trad repertoire.

items.title: Bleaching Bones
items.description: Released March 16, 2018, this album features traditional Irish songs performed in unaccompanied 4-part harmony by Dublin-based group Landless (Lily Power, Meabh Meir, Ruth Clinton, and Sinead Lynch). The collection includes both traditional ballads and contemporary interpretations of Irish folk music.

items.title: Leitrim to London
items.description: Released in 2002 on Phaeton Records, this album showcases Leitrim-born and London-based fiddle player Brian Rooney performing traditional Irish fiddle music. The recording features Brian McGrath on piano and John Blake on guitar providing sensitive accompaniment to Rooney's distinctive playing style.

items.title: Here and There
items.description: A 1998 release on Green Linnet Records featuring Séamus Connolly, a celebrated master of traditional Irish fiddle music. The album presents a collection of assorted Irish traditional tunes performed with sensitivity and musicianship, showcasing Connolly's mastery of the instrument and his deep knowledge of the Irish music tradition.

items.title: Crazy Old World
items.description: A 1997 release of original compositions by Paul McDonald, featuring his own musical creations. The album showcases traditional Irish musical forms interpreted through McDonald's compositional lens.
items.notes: Limited information available for this album. Detailed track listing and album art could not be located.

items.title: Invernalia; Sol Invictus
items.description: Released November 12, 2013, this album by Spanish Celtic folk group Invernalia presents Irish traditional music performed with European folk sensibilities. Formed in 2009 in Barcelona, Invernalia combines members with expertise in Irish flute, uilleann pipes, whistles, and accordion alongside traditional guitar, delivering a unique cross-cultural interpretation of Irish trad.

items.title: The Windy Turn
items.description: A 2000 release featuring flute player Dan Healy from Ballymote, County Sligo and fiddle player Ciaran O'Raghallaigh from Cavan performing a generous selection of 18 traditional Irish tunes, primarily from Sligo, interspersed with O'Carolan pieces. The album features Slow airs given an unusually prominent place, with O'Raghallaigh's choice of viola adding special poignancy to these performances.

items.title: The Open Road
items.description: Released June 26, 2009, this album by Galway-based flute player Steph Geremia features 12 tracks of traditional Irish music with exceptional guest musicians. The album showcases Geremia's fluent flute playing accompanied by an ensemble including accordion, bodhrán, bouzouki, fiddle, harp, percussion, and guitar, creating a rich tapestry of traditional Irish sound.
trackList: The Garsun who beat his father

items.title: Solas
items.description: The 2005 album 'Waiting for an Echo' by American Irish traditional music ensemble Solas showcases their distinctive blend of traditional Irish tunes and original compositions. This 13-track album features the band's signature sound recorded across two continents in hotel rooms, dressing rooms, and studios, demonstrating their versatility and musicianship.
trackList: The Hanover Reel / John James' Reel / The Copperplate
Tom Sullivan's / Mick's Polka / The Newmarket Polka

items.title: The Coast Road
items.description: Released March 5, 2012 on Cló Iar-Chonnacht, this album features fiddle/whistle/banjo player Karen Ryan accompanied by Pete Quinn on piano and keyboard, with special guest musicians. The 21-track collection showcases traditional Irish jigs, reels, slow airs, and songs with a contemporary ensemble arrangement that honors the traditional roots of the music.

items.title: Welcome Paddy Home
items.description: A 2015 release from the Irish Corner Folk Group based in Basque Country, Spain, featuring traditional Irish music and songs. The album represents the group's celebration of Irish musical heritage and their passion for sharing the warmth and character of Irish traditional music with audiences across Europe.
items.notes: Limited information available for this album. Full details could not be located.

items.title: The Road West
items.description: Released in 2001, this 13-track album features entirely original compositions by Máirtín O'Connor, the renowned Galway-based accordion player. The album showcases O'Connor's creative approach to traditional Irish musical forms, presenting original reels, jigs, and waltzes that honor the traditional idiom while demonstrating his compositional imagination.

items.title: A Woman's Heart
items.description: Released in July 1992, this groundbreaking compilation album features 12 traditional Irish songs performed by six of Ireland's most accomplished female singers and musicians. Originally produced for RTE, the album became a landmark release in Irish traditional music, showcasing the vocal talents and musicianship of Maura O'Connell, Frances Black, Dolores Keane, Sharon Shannon, Eleanor McEvoy, and Mary Black.
trackList: Only A Woman's Heart - Mary Black with Eleanor McEvoy
I Hear You Breathing In - Eleanor McEvoy

items.title: On Love and Other Murder Mysteries
items.description: A 2013 EP release by Carrick-on-Shannon singer-songwriter Ailie Blunnie, featuring original music, lyrics, and arrangements. The third track, 'How much do you love me?', won the UK International Songwriting Competition in 2014 in the Love Songs category, demonstrating the quality of Blunnie's songwriting.
items.notes: 3rd track 'How much do you love me?' won UK International Songwriting Competition 2014.

items.title: Our Dear Dark Mountain with the Sky Over It
items.description: A 15-track 2014 release documenting Irish traditional music from the Sliabh Beagh region of North Monaghan and East Fermanagh. This project reunites musical repertoire collected in the region during the 19th and early 20th century with contemporary traditional musicians from Counties Monaghan and Fermanagh, arising from the PhD research of Seán McElwain.

items.description: A 2008 release by Mick Lavelle, the legendary Irish singer and storyteller from Westport, County Mayo. Known for his distinctive voice and ability to tell stories through song, Lavelle's work represents the heart of Irish traditional music and comedy.
items.notes: Mick Lavelle (1930-2013) was a beloved Irish entertainer. Limited album details available.

items.title: Séamus Egan
items.description: Released in 1990 on Shanachie Records, Séamus Egan's self-titled album 'A Week in January' showcases the multi-talented musician's fluency across flute, banjo, mandolin, and other traditional Irish instruments. This 9-track album features Egan's distinctive interpretations of traditional tunes alongside original compositions.

items.title: Carrying the Tune
items.description: Released in 2012, this album features master flute player Kevin Crawford with an ensemble of exceptional musicians: John Doyle on guitar and bouzouki, Brian Morrissey on bodhrán, and Mick Conneely on bouzouki. The 14-track recording showcases traditional Irish music from County Clare and beyond, highlighting the group's musical interplay and understanding of traditional repertoire.

items.title: The Last Bell
items.description: Released August 21, 2014, this 12-track album by the Alan Kelly Gang features a mix of 8 instrumental traditional Irish music pieces and 4 vocal tracks. Recorded live in Rathcoole, Dublin with producer Manus Lunny from Capercaille, the album captures the energy and musicianship of Dublin's traditional music scene.

items.title: Dublin Made Me
items.description: Released in 2009 on Na Píobairí Uilleann, this album features traditional Irish music performed on uilleann pipes (Seán McKeon) and fiddle (Liam O'Connor). The 15-track recording showcases reels, jigs, hornpipes, marches, airs, and set dances from the traditional Irish music repertoire, demonstrating the musicianship of two of Ireland's finest traditional musicians.

items.description: A 2010 release documenting sea shanties and maritime music performed by various artists from Ireland, the Netherlands, and the UK at the Rosses Point Shanty Festival. This collection celebrates maritime heritage through traditional shanties and seafaring songs, showcasing the cultural exchange of folk music traditions.
items.notes: Limited information available for this album. The Rosses Point Shanty Festival began in 2010 and has continued as the Wild Atlantic Shanty Festival.

items.title: Apples in Winter
items.description: Released in April 2016, this album features traditional Irish music performed on uilleann pipes (David Power) and fiddle (Willie Kelly). The two musicians, both masters of their respective instruments, showcase their musicianship through a collection of reels, jigs, hornpipes, and airs, demonstrating the depth and beauty of the Irish traditional music repertoire.
items.notes: Album features opening track 'The Flax in Bloom' and includes traditional jigs and reels.

items.description: A collection of Irish traditional tunes released in 2000 by The Arts Council & Co. This compilation features various artists performing a diverse selection of traditional Irish music.

items.title: Fear an Bhata and Captain O'Kane (O'Carolan)
items.description: Paul Dooley's interpretation of traditional airs on wire-strung harp, featuring 'Fear an Bhata' and 'Captain O'Kane.' This recording showcases Dooley's mastery of the medieval harper style with metal-strung harp in 2015.

items.title: CEOL 2018
items.description: Curated by Eoghan McDermott for RTÉ 2FM and Conradh na Gaeilge in 2018, this compilation features Irish artists performing their songs in the Irish language. The album was distributed to all schools in Ireland with download codes.

items.title: The Martin Family & the McGovern Family
items.description: A recording from 2013 featuring the Martin Family and the McGovern Family performing Irish traditional music. This album captures the family tradition of Irish music in County Leitrim.

items.title: How's About Now?
items.description: Tiernan Courell's debut solo EP, released June 24, 2022, features the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland graduate on flute and whistle. The 6-track collection showcases traditional Irish dance music arrangements with Craig Baxter on bodhran, Ali Hutton on guitar, Rory Matheson on piano, and James Lindsay on bass.
trackList: Reels: The Steeplechase/The Eel In The Sink/Hasbullas Big Day Out!
Jigs: Father Spodo Komodo's Trip to Togo/The Day the Ice Age Ended/How's About Now?
Hop Jigs: The Boys Of Ballisodare/The Surround
Reels: Dave Normaway MacDonald's Wedding/Eanach Mhic Coilin/St. Kilda Wedding

items.title: A Selection of Music from Racket Records 1997-2022
items.description: Released October 23, 2022, this 25-year celebration compilation from Rackety Records features 16 tracks of traditional Irish music. The album showcases acclaimed musicians including John Carty on banjo and fiddle, Michael McGoldrick on flute, Matt Molloy on flute, and Brian Rooney on fiddle.

items.title: Lightweights & Gentlemen
items.description: Lau's debut album, recorded in December 2006 and originally released in 2007, features Kris Drever on guitar and vocals, Aidan O'Rourke on fiddle, and Martin Green on accordion. The 2012 remaster includes 11 live tracks from the Bongo Club Edinburgh, showcasing modern Scottish and Celtic folk traditions.

items.title: Ré Órga
items.description: Released June 12, 2023 on Raelach Records, this album features Geraldine Cotter on piano and reed organ performing 15 compositions blending Irish traditional music with jazz and classical influences. The recording brings together an ensemble of Scottish and Irish musicians recorded across 2018-2023.
trackList: An Gaoth Aniar Aneas and Patsy Geary's
The Blackberry Blossom and Miss Thornton

items.title: 3'oh
items.description: Released July 13, 2019, this Mayo-Sligo trio collaboration features David Munnelly on accordion, Joseph McNulty on fiddle, and Shane McGowan on guitar. The 10-track album showcases traditional Irish reels, jigs, and original compositions in a dynamic traditional setting.
trackList: The Bog Carrot - Mossey Banks - Swallows tail
Seanamhac Tube Station - Rakes Of Clonmel
Factory Smoke - Polly Put the Kettle on

items.title: The Whistle Blower
items.description: Released October 30, 2018, this innovative album and multimedia project features Cormac Breatnach on whistles and vocals, Daire Bracken on fiddle and electronic loops, and Martin Tourish on accordion. The work serves as an artistic response to the 1976 Sallins Mail Train Robbery, combining traditional music with contemporary production.
trackList: On Board the Cork and Dublin Travelling Post Office While Justice Sleeps
See No Evil, Hear No Evil, Speak No Evil
The Beginning and the End: Aftermath

items.title: Lovely Leitrim (12 Songs)
items.description: A compilation celebrating County Leitrim featuring 12 traditional Irish ballads and folk songs. The album includes the iconic 'Lovely Leitrim,' a #1 hit in Ireland from 1966 written by NYPD officer Philip Fitzpatrick, a native of Aughavas in County Leitrim, performed as a graceful waltz.
